articles.title: In China, swapping out an EV battery is as fast as filling up a gas tank
articles.summary: In China, some EV owners have the option to swap out a low battery for a fully charged one, instead of waiting at a charging station. The whole process only takes about three minutes.

articles.title: Prosecutor Withdraws From Trump Team’s Investigation of Ex-CIA Director John O. Brennan
articles.summary: A career Justice Department lawyer, Maria Medetis Long, in Miami is said to have raised concerns about whether the evidence justified moving forward with a bid to prosecute John O. Brennan.

articles.title: Strait of Hormuz Is Open During Cease-Fire in Lebanon, U.S. and Iran Say
articles.summary: Statements from President Trump and Iran aimed to raise confidence in the safety of the waterway, but shipping experts said risks remained.

articles.title: Federal Appeals Court Opens Door to Moving Trans Inmates Under Trump Gender Order
articles.summary: A three-judge panel gave a group of 17 transgender women a few weeks to seek further recourse in court before their transfer to men’s facilities could take effect.

articles.title: Stocks Extend Rally as Tensions Ease Over War in Iran
articles.summary: The S&P 500’s rise on Friday caps a striking three-week streak, powered by investors’ optimism about the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and strong corporate earnings.

articles.title: Logjam of U.S. immigration applications puts millions at greater risk of deportation
articles.summary: An NPR analysis shows how immigrants' attempts to live or work legally in the U.S. are caught in a bureaucratic morass.

articles.title: Carney Courts Investors to Reduce Canada’s Economic Dependence on U.S.
articles.summary: Prime Minister Mark Carney has set up a meeting and a new office to speed project approvals and make Canada more attractive to investors.

articles.title: Gasoline could drop below $4 in coming days
articles.summary: Gasoline costs should start to fall soon, although a full recovery to pre-war prices is expected to take months. That's assuming that peace holds and traffic flows resume through the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Lutnick Says Canada Trade Deal Needs to Be Reworked Ahead of Talks
articles.summary: Howard Lutnick, President Trump’s commerce secretary, derided Canada’s trade strategy and said a North American deal needed to be reworked.

articles.title: Trump Is Urged to Act on Iranian Site Feared Impervious to Airstrikes
articles.summary: Little is known about Pickaxe Mountain, but some experts say it illustrates the impossibility of relying on force alone to prevent Iran from acquiring a nuclear bomb.

articles.title: Why scientists are nervous about fungi
articles.summary: They can pose a threat to human health — yeast infections are but one example. Scientists say not enough attention is paid to their ability to develop resistance to medications that treat them.

articles.title: The U.S. blockade continues despite Iran's announcement the Strait of Hormuz is open
articles.summary: Iran's foreign minister declared the Strait of Hormuz is open, following the start of an Israel-Lebanon ceasefire. President Trump swiftly responded that the U.S. naval blockade on Iran will continue.

articles.title: Israel starts a tense ceasefire in Lebanon. And, Trump nominates a new CDC director.
articles.summary: A 10-day ceasefire to pause fighting between Israel and Hezb

articles.title: The 27-Year-Old Diplomat Waging Trump’s Cultural War With Europe
articles.summary: Five years out of college, Samuel Samson has driven the Trump administration’s push to upend America’s postwar relationship with Europe.

articles.title: Who says they have no fear of the Trump administration? The quiz knows
articles.summary: Also: If you know what Eric Swalwell looks like, you'll get at least one question correct.

articles.title: Human rights groups raise alarm over fate of Salvadorans deported from U.S.
articles.summary: Migrants deported from the U.S. routinely disappear into El Salvador's prisons the moment they land or in the weeks that follow. Many remain incommunicado from family and lawyers for  years.

articles.title: Do less, ask for more: How to make life easier as a working parent
articles.summary: Going back to work after having a baby can be overwhelming. You're juggling all the emotions of being a new parent while getting up to speed at your job. Tips to help you make a smooth transition.

articles.title: Six months after ceasefire with Israel, people in Gaza say recovery hasn't even begun
articles.summary: Six months into a ceasefire that promised an end to the war and a surge of aid for Gaza, people say recovery hasn't even yet begun.

articles.title: UN looks for a short-term solution for moving fertilizer through the Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: The U.N. looks for a quick solution to get cargo ships with fertilizer through the Strait of Hormuz, to prevent a global food crisis.

articles.title: How the conflict in Iran is testing the U.S.-European alliance
articles.summary: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with correspondents Greg Myre and Rob Schmitz about how the Iran war is affecting the U.S. alliance with Europe.

articles.title: Israeli and Palestinian activists share a vision for peace in Gaza
articles.summary: Maoz Inon's parents were killed by Hamas in the Oct. 7 attacks. Aziz Abu Sarah's brother died after being tortured in an Israeli military prison. Their new book is The Future Is Peace.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
